name: Documentation Update
description: Survey existing docs, approve the update scope, draft updated content, validate all links and code examples, gate, then polish. Best for API docs, READMEs, guides, and inline comments.

steps.title: Survey Existing Docs
steps.prompt_template:
You are a technical writer performing a documentation audit.

Documentation update target: {{feature_description}}
Repositories in scope: {{repo_list}}

## Your task
Survey all documentation related to the update target.

## Required sections
1. **Files to Update** — table: file path | current state | what's wrong or missing | priority (High/Medium/Low)
2. **Style & Voice Reference** — identify the existing tone, tense, and formatting conventions from the best-written existing doc in the repo.
3. **Link Inventory** — all internal and external links in the docs that will change. Flag any already-broken links.
4. **Code Examples** — list all code examples in scope. Flag examples that are likely outdated.
5. **Proposed Scope** — a conservative list of exactly which files will be touched and what will change. Do not include nice-to-haves.

## Stop condition
You are done when all 5 sections are present and every file in 'Files to Update' has a stated priority.

steps.title: Approve Documentation Scope
steps.prompt_template: Review the Survey artifact from 's-survey'. Approve the proposed scope to begin drafting. Redirect to narrow or expand the scope — your feedback will adjust the draft's focus. Cancel to abort.

steps.title: Draft Updated Documentation
steps.prompt_template:
You are a technical writer updating documentation.

Update target: {{feature_description}}
Survey artifact: [attached — from s-survey]
Gate scope feedback: {{gate_feedback}}

## Rules
1. Write in the style and voice documented in the survey's 'Style & Voice Reference' section. Match tense, heading style, and tone exactly.
2. Update ONLY the files in the approved 'Proposed Scope'.
3. Add or update code examples where they improve clarity — mark new examples with an `<!-- EXAMPLE: new -->` comment.
4. Do NOT break existing markdown anchors (e.g., `#section-name`) without updating all references to them.
5. Do NOT update links that are not in the survey's Link Inventory unless they are obviously broken.

## On Retry (only when retry feedback is present)
If the following feedback is non-empty, this is attempt {{iteration}} of {{max_iterations}} — validation failed previously:
{{retry_feedback}}
Focus exclusively on resolving what the feedback describes (e.g. broken links, failing examples); do not redo unrelated work. If the feedback is empty, this is the first attempt — ignore this section.

## Output artifact
- List of files written
- New or updated sections (summary)
- Code examples added/updated (list)
- Links updated (list)

## Stop condition
You are done when all files in the approved scope have been written.

steps.title: Validate Links & Code Examples
steps.prompt_template:
You are a documentation QA engineer performing a technical validation pass.

Draft artifact: [attached — from s-draft]
Survey link inventory: [attached — from s-survey]

## Validation checklist
1. **Internal links** — verify every internal markdown link resolves to an existing heading or file. List broken links.
2. **External links** — check the links from the survey's Link Inventory that were updated. Verify they resolve correctly.
3. **Code examples** — for every `<!-- EXAMPLE: new -->` example added: attempt to compile or run it. Report pass/fail per example.
4. **Anchor integrity** — list any heading that was renamed or removed; flag if there are stale references elsewhere in the docs.

## Output artifact
- Broken internal links: list (should be empty)
- Broken external links: list
- Code example results: example | PASS / FAIL / NOT TESTED
- Stale anchors: list
- **Verdict: VALID / INVALID**

## Stop condition
You are done when all 4 checklist items are assessed and a Verdict is stated.

verifier.instructions: Decide whether the documentation update is correct and complete. Base your verdict primarily on the validation results (broken links, failing code examples, stale anchors, and the VALID/INVALID verdict); treat any harness output above as secondary. Return "pass" only if there are no broken internal links and no failing code examples; otherwise return "fail" with a concise, actionable reason naming exactly what to fix.

steps.title: Review Draft Documentation
steps.prompt_template: Review the Draft artifact (s-draft) and the Validation report (s-validate). If Validation shows INVALID, redirect to fix the broken items first. If VALID: approve to begin the final polish pass. Cancel to abort.

steps.title: Polish & Finalise
steps.prompt_template:
You are a technical writer doing a final polish pass.

Gate feedback: {{gate_feedback}}
Draft artifact: [attached — from s-draft]
Validation artifact: [attached — from s-validate]

## Tasks
1. Apply all reviewer feedback from the gate decision.
2. Fix all INVALID items from the validation report (broken links, failing examples).
3. Final style pass: consistent heading capitalisation, no trailing whitespace, no double blank lines.
4. Write a CHANGELOG entry summarising the documentation changes for this feature.

## Output artifact
- Gate feedback items resolved: list
- Validation issues resolved: list
- CHANGELOG entry (copy-paste ready)

## Stop condition
You are done when all gate feedback and validation issues are resolved and the CHANGELOG entry is written.
